Read more about SAP Build Code here.

 

Orlando
  • BTP117 | Generative AI–based code development from SAP Build Code
  • BTP118 | Discover generative AI-based code development from SAP Build Code
  • BTP119 | Application development with SAP Build and SAP Build Code: Road map review
Barcelona
  • BTP138 | Generative AI–based code development from SAP Build Code
Virtual
  • BTP224v | Extend SAP S/4HANA with SAP Build and SAP Build Code

 

Honourable Mention:

Accelerate Financial Planning & Analysis

You: Working and collaborating across the organization gets overwhelming, as Financial Planning & Analysis is not a one-person or even one-department job; the CEO, the offices of CFO, and the other stakeholders across the business all hold key business data and processes crucial to FP&A, but it seems like each area has their own rules and data in different places. This causes delays in accessing the real-time data, impeding proactive planning and agile decision-making.

How SAP BTP can help: With the power of data integration, visualization, and predictive analytics, SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning serves as a reporting, analytics, and planning tool in one. It allows business users to access current real-time data in a simple and timely manner, eliminating delays in the planning process. Your financial planning projects can be fast-tracked with SAP’s prebuilt business accelerators, and activating predictive analytics with Machine Learning capabilities is as easy as a single click. Read more on SAP’s Financial Planning and Analysis solutions here.

 

To wrap up

SAP Business Technology Platform provides organizations with the tools and capabilities to thrive in the digital age. Through the lens of Generative AI, self-service portals, and developer productivity tools, SAP BTP not only addresses challenges but also unlocks opportunities for growth and innovation. It's not just about staying ahead; it's about leading the charge towards excellence. Now that these three new use cases have piqued your interest, visit SAP Business Technology Platform In Action community page for the full list of SAP BTP Use Cases and other resources. 

With a lineup of industry experts, hands-on workshops, and networking opportunities, Sapphire 2024 is not to be missed. We look forward to seeing you in Orlando, Barcelona, or virtually and sharing more about what SAP Business Technology Platform can do for you and your organization. Get ready to unlock your business's full potential and explore the possibilities of the intelligent enterprise.

 

Additional Resources

  • HCM 102 | Technology strategy and road map for SAP SuccessFactors solutions session to learn more about how SAP BTP works with SAP SuccessFactors.

 

*The state of AI in 2023: Generative AI’s breakout year. (2023, August 1). McKinsey & Company. https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/quantumblack/our-insights/the-state-of-ai-in-2023-generative-ais-breakout-year

**Murty, R. N. (2022, November 1). How much time and energy do we waste toggling between applications? Harvard Business Review. https://hbr.org/2022/08/how-much-time-and-energy-do-we-waste-toggling-between-applications

***IDC: Generative AI and turnkey development platforms can improve developer productivity. (n.d.). SAP. https://www.sap.com/canada/documents/2024/01/fe86d503-a57e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html

entry.title: What's new for Workspaces in SAP Build Work Zone?
entry.content:
Recently new features were released to streamline the creation process and the member handling of workspaces in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition. Among these features you will find:

  • a new workspace creation dialog
  • the removal of external workspaces as a separate workspace type  
  • a consistent user experience between private and public workspaces
  • expanded access control concepts: external users can now access public and private workspaces as members

See also the information in the What's New Viewer from April 18, 2024. 

These are the major innovations for workspaces:

Removal of External Workspaces
You cannot create anymore “External Workspaces”. When you create a new workspace, you can choose between creating a public workspace or a private workspace:

What are public workspaces? Workspaces that all users can view, join and collaborate in. Members can be invited to join or they can search for the workspace using the search functionality or by browsing on the workspaces screen and then request to join. Also non-members can join a public workspace - depending on the settings defined by the administrator.

What are private workspaces? Workspaces that only those users can join, who are invited to the workspace. In general, private workspaces are hidden and can't be found with the search functionality. If you're not invited to a private workspace, you can't see it on the workspaces screen unless the workspace administrator has defined the setting to Allow users to request to join this workspace.

Migration of External Workspaces to Private Workspaces
Existing external workspaces will be migrated automatically to private workspaces. An external warning icon will be added to the workspace header to indicate that the content of this workspace is visible to external users:

Handling of External Users/Members
External users can now be invited to both private and public workspaces. External users are guests of an organization, and they should not have access to any confidential or internal information. Before external users can access the system, they must be created on Identity Authentication and be provisioned by the Identity Provisioning service to SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition. For more information, see About External Users.

This blog post guides you through the steps to create a private workspace. In general the creation flow is streamlined, and there is no big difference between creating private or public workspaces.

How to create a workspace?

  1. When creating a new workspace, choose "Private Workspace" or "Public Workspace". In this blog post we will focus on creating a private workspace:

2. Enter a unique name and a description. Select one of the templates or you can select No Template. 

Note: If the workspace is public, the description is visible for everybody in the search. When the workspace is private, the description is visible for members only after they’ve been invited to the workspace or join the workspace.

You can define if the workspace is activated on creation, or you activate it later.

Define the following settings:

  • External users can access the workspace and can be invited as members.
  • Allow all members to invite users. When not selected, only workspace admins can invite users.
  • Allow users to request to join this workspace (for private workspaces) or Allow others to join this workspace (for public workspaces).

 3. Click on Next to define the member participation settings. They can be read-only, limited or full. You also define the task, upload and approval policy:

4. Click Finish and the workspace is created.

5. You can change each of the settings in the edit mode of the workspace:

6. If needed you can change the member participation setting, for example you change the collaboration level from Full to Limited:

Additional Features for Public Workspaces

Collaboration Levels for Non-Members
A non-member visibility is available for workspaces to give non-members access to the workspace content. Workspace admins have the choice: (1) to give non-members the same collaboration settings as members or (2) to use a new read-only mode for non-members. This will hide the different membership & collaboration related features for the non-members across the workspace and its content. 

Access Control Settings 
Public workspaces now offer expanded access option to non-members, and offer these access options:

  • Specific users or user lists
  • Specific roles (for site admins)
  • All internal users
  • All external users (when enabled)
  • All users

If you need additional information on workspaces, you can access the SAP Help Portal:

How to create a workspace? 
How to manage workspaces? 
Workspace Settings

If you have more questions on SAP Build Work Zone, visit our FAQ page on SAP Community.

More Information on new Features and Functions

  • Check out the roadmap for standard & advanced edition.
  • You can find more information in the What is new Viewer for standard edition and advanced edition.
  • The availability date of the new features on the preview systems and the information about how to use them can be found in the What's New on Preview Systems.

Learn more

SAP Build Work Zone on: sap.com / SAP Community
Discovery Center missions: standard / advanced edition
SAP Build Work Zone on SAP PartnerEdge

Follow SAP Build on Social Media and Join the SAP Builders Group

  • Twitter (hashtag #SAPBuild)
  • LinkedIn

Join also the SAP Builders group and engage with a community of peers to unleash innovation by building apps, process automations, and creating business sites.

entry.title: What’s new with SAP Build Work Zone guided experiences
entry.content:
Navigating complex business processes can be daunting, often leading to inefficiencies, mistakes, and user frustration. To address these challenges, both SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition, and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone offer a differentiated capability called guided experiences. This feature enables business users to simplify complex business processes into self-service journeys that are easy to follow and execute. In this blog, we will explore what a guided experience is, how it works, the latest enhancements, and a couple of real-life customer examples.

An overview of guided experiences

Guided experiences are designed to simplify multi-step business processes by breaking them down into manageable and intuitive steps. Each step can be enriched with various types of content, including videos, documents, forms, tasks, UI cards, and apps to provide users with all the necessary information to complete each step. Steps can be classified as optional or mandatory, ensuring critical tasks are completed while allowing flexibility for other actions. Users don't need to worry about finishing all the steps at once as the progress is saved within Work Zone, enabling them to resume from where they left off. 

Guided experiences can be used for various complex processes such as parental leave, benefits enrollment, employee relocation, new hires onboarding or suppliers onboarding, training and enablement, and browsing and purchasing electronic equipment.

New hires onboarding guided experience

Supplier enablement guided experience

Create, modify, and extend guided experiences with low-code

Creating a guided experience in SAP Build Work Zone is a straightforward process. It can be done through low-code and without IT dependency! The building process is streamlined through a wizard-based layout, supported by different types of content, such as videos, documents, UI cards, and apps, which can also be found on other workpages. This enables the reuse of pre-built and custom content as well as SAP-delivered content, boosting efficiency. The wizard layout can be added to any workpage within a workspace or in the overall menu. The low-code approach also allows business experts to quickly build and deploy guided experiences without requiring extensive technical know-how. 

Set up a guided experience through a wizard

Looking ahead in Q3, business experts can create a multi-step process in SAP Build Process Automation. They can then use it as a process template called guided process to create a guided experience in Work Zone, which includes pre-built content from SAP SuccessFactors. This eliminates the need to recreate the process from scratch and accelerates the entire process from building to rolling out the experience for production.

Real-life customer examples using guided experiences

A customer has integrated SAP Build Work Zone with SAP Concur to simplify business travel planning. When employees need to plan travels to a production facility or an overseas office, they are guided through a series of questions to ensure necessary preparations are made, such as the need for an office workstation or proper approval. This streamlines the travel request process. This customer is also looking to use guided experiences as an intake form to collect information and flow this information into SAP Build Process Automation, then to SAP Task Center, triggering notifications and task updates for business users. 

Another customer uses guided experiences to develop comprehensive learning journeys on different subjects, such as IT and leadership, tailored to the specific needs of different audiences. These learning journeys incorporate both structured and unstructured content from various sources, such as SAP SuccessFactors Learning and LinkedIn Learning. This approach ensures that employees receive targeted training in a well-structured and easily accessible way, thus improving their overall learning experience.

Value delivered through guided experiences

The guided experiences in SAP Build Work Zone are changing the way businesses manage complex processes. By breaking down these processes into easy-to-understand visual elements, business experts can rapidly build, modify, implement, and deploy the experience to improve efficiency, accuracy, and user satisfaction. This also helps improve end users' overall experience, allowing them to accomplish tasks more efficiently.

Learn more

Visit SAP Build Work Zone, start your free product trial, and engage in the Community to learn more.

entry.title: Implement side-by-side extensibility with SAP Build Apps and SAP Process Automation in BTP onPremise
entry.content:
SAP Build is SAP’s low-code solution, aimed at enabling business users to effortlessly construct business applications. It integrates SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Work Zone into a cohesive development environment, introducing fresh innovations for swift app creation, process automation, and business site generation.

Scenario:
A business Partner(BP) fetched from S4Hana on-premise system. Then BP Tax no will change in BTP App . Then Approver will Approve or Reject. If Approver approved, then BP Tax no will update in backend.

Systems:
In this topic we used below Technologies and Systems.
1. SAP Build Apps
2. SAP Process Automation
3. SAP S4Hana On-Premise as backend

Steps:
1. API from S4HANA system should be exposed to get consumed.

2. Then build a App in BTP.

 

3. Get the Entity from Backend destination and bind it to Data of the App.

 

4. Build the App with 2 pages. First page will show the BP Header data in List format.


Second Page with Input field.

 

5. Create an Action to post the changes using API.

 

 

6. Create a Workflow using Business Process Automation in BTP.

In this simple workflow it will be trigger first from APP after Save button. Then it will go to the approver . If Approver approve, update will happen in BP.

7. Deploy the app in SAP Build Work Zone.

 


Test results:
1. Open the App from SAP Build App preview or SAP Build Work zone. The BP no and Tax Id fetched from backend on the first page.

 

 


2. After click on respective line, 2nd page will display. Give the Tax no for update.

 


3. After Save , Workflow will be trigger.

 

 

4. It will shown in the My Inbox App of approvers with updated Tax id.

 

5. If User approve, Update will be triggered from Workflow. Workflow will be completed.

 

6. BP will be updated with new Tax no.

 

 

This document has been prepared only a summary to showcase the side by side extensibility. There ae some configurations need to be done in both SAP Build Apps, BTP and SAP Build work zone.
